quoteFirst: At last everything was ready, and he was very pleased with his appearance. The big leather riding-boots that went with the dress were just a little too large for him, and he could only find one of the two horse-pistols, but, on the whole, he was quite satisfied, and at a
quoteLast: he glided out of the wainscoting and crept down the corridor. On reaching the room occupied by the twins, which I should mention was called the Blue Bed Chamber on account of the color of its hangings, he found the door just ajar.

quoteFirst: On the day of the robbery he had started out as usual--possibly a little earlier than was customary--and the bills and other securities in his possession represented considerably more than £15,000. quoteLast: , being then, without doubt, in possession of cash to the full value of the bills negotiated. After that, Mr. Lyster said, yesterday's report was that nothing more had been heard of him.

quoteLast: . It then moves again to the east, and having reached its greatest easterly excursion at half-past eight in the evening, returns to the west, and attains its greatest westerly excursion at eleven, as at starting.

quoteFirst: "Jonas he whipped up the horse and we got there pretty quick, and I'll be bound if the Ashby house hadn't got stuck fast right on the track, and stir it one way or another they couldn't. They'd been there since
quoteLast: , pulling and hauling,--and the men was all hoarse with yelling, and the cars had come from both ways and met there,--one each side of the crossing,--and the passengers was walking about, scolding and swearing,--and somebody'd gone and lit up a gre't bonfire. You never see such a sight in all your life! I happened to look up at the old house, and there were them two top windows that used to look over to our place, and they had caught the shine of the firelight, and made the poor old thing look as if it was scared to death.
title: Deephaven and Selected Stories & Sketches

quoteFirst: But it was not in human endurance to keep on in the midst of the burning fort. This splendid last effort was short. At
quoteLast: , Anderson ceased firing and raised a white flag. Negotiations followed ending in terms of surrender--Anderson to be allowed to remove his garrison to the fleet lying idle beyond the bar and to salute the flag of the United States before taking it down.
